summary: Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > China Doll shines; tight set with soaring Bird Song
blurb: The concert opens with a bright Bird Song and Deal before exploding into a powerhouse Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > China Doll sequence. Jerry’s inventive solos, Brent’s tasteful keys, and the classic Drumz‑>Space race drive an electrifying second set that rolls into The Wheel and Not Fade Away. A heartfelt Baby Blue closes the night.
review:
On October 28, 1984 at Berkeley Community Theater the Dead delivered a high‑energy performance that still feels fresh. The first set builds nicely with Bird Song’s dreamy spacey vibes and a solid Deal, then launches into one of the era’s most memorable sequences – Terrapin Station seamlessly flowing into Playing In The Band and crushing into China Doll. Jerry Garcia riffs on J.S. Bach during Space, wails through each song, and even when his vocals dip they stay strong enough to carry the emotion of Terrapin Station. Brent Mydland’s relaxed yet edgy keyboards sit perfectly under the mix, while Phil Lesh provides a “on” bass foundation that locks the band together. The second set is a masterclass in Dead improvisation: a tight Jam leads into a top‑notch Drumz > Space race that propels The Wheel and Not Fade Away, culminating in an emotional encore of It’s All Over Now Baby Blue. Across all available sources the show is praised for its crisp sound, tight flow, and standout moments that make it a must‑listen snapshot of the mid‑80s Dead.

For listeners seeking the best audio experience, the 24‑bit Rob Lamarre soundboard capture (gd1984-10-28.145578.aud.lamarre.berger.flac24) offers crystal‑clear separation and balanced levels, preserving every nuance of that unforgettable night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Terrapin>Playing in the Band>China Doll sequence, Bird Song jam, Drumz>Space race and overall tight set flow. No negative comments appear, indicating strong consensus of an exceptional performance.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp separation, balanced levels and no drop‑outs; showcases the full detail of the performance.

key_highlights: Terrapin Station > Playing In The Band > China Doll sequence is a high‑energy centerpiece.
Bird Song and Deal provide dreamy, soaring moments in the first set.
Drumz > Space race into The Wheel and Not Fade Away drives an electrifying second set.
Emotional encore with It’s All Over Now Baby Blue caps the night.
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers inventive solos, from Bach‑inspired riffs in Space to soaring leads on Bird Song and a spot‑on tone on Terrapin Station; vocals are solid though occasionally rough.
band_performance.Phil: Provides profound, on‑point bass work that underpins the band's interplay throughout the show.
band_performance.Brent: Offers relaxed yet edgy keyboard tones that complement the jams and keep the harmonic landscape rich.
band_performance.Mickey: Drumming is top‑notch, especially in the second‑set jam and Drumz>Space race, driving the momentum with precision.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in tight with Mickey, delivering classic drum interplay that fuels The Wheel and Not Fade Away.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
